senses.definition: at an earlier time

examples.x: Guests must submit dietary requests beforehand.
The speaker emailed her notes beforehand.
Parking permits expire at noon; renew them beforehand.
He always reads meeting agendas beforehand.
The system requires login credentials beforehand.

senses.definition: referring to an event or occurrence that is about to happen very soon

examples.x: The company's CEO promised that forthcoming changes would lead to improved efficiency.
Despite her best efforts, she found no forthcoming solution to the problem.
The politician remained tight-lipped about any forthcoming policy initiatives.
The author hinted at forthcoming revelations in the next installment of the book series.
The team's coach remained optimistic about their forthcoming match despite recent setbacks.

senses.definition: in the order in which events, actions, or items occurred, following a timeline or sequence

examples.x: The events in the history book are presented chronologically.
Please list the tasks chronologically to ensure proper prioritization.
The timeline illustrates the development of technology chronologically.
The professor explained the scientific discoveries chronologically.
The documents are organized chronologically for easy reference.

senses.definition: for an unspecified period of time

examples.x: The event has been postponed indefinitely due to unforeseen circumstances.
She decided to leave her job and travel, with plans to stay abroad indefinitely.
The construction project is on hold indefinitely until funding is secured.
The team will continue their research indefinitely until conclusive results are obtained.
The road closure will last indefinitely as repairs are more extensive than anticipated.

senses.definition: up until now

examples.x: To date, we have received over 100 applications for the job opening.
This is the most successful product launch we've had to date.
To date, no one has been able to solve the mystery surrounding the missing artifact.
Their relationship has been going strong to date, with no signs of slowing down.
We have raised $10,000 for charity to date, but our goal is to reach $20,000 by the end of the month.

senses.definition: for a limited period, usually until a certain condition changes

examples.x: For the time being, I am focusing on my studies before deciding on my career path.
She is serving as the interim manager for the time being until a permanent replacement is found.
We'll use this office for the time being until our new space is ready.
I'll take care of this task for the time being, but we should discuss a more permanent solution.
The current arrangement is acceptable for the time being, but we'll need a long-term plan.

senses.definition: without a fixed schedule or pattern

examples.x: I like to visit my hometown from time to time to catch up with old friends.
I enjoy going for walks in the park from time to time to clear my mind.
She visits her grandparents' house from time to time to catch up with them.
We like to indulge in dessert from time to time, but we try to eat healthily most days.
From time to time, I like to switch up my workout routine to keep things interesting.

senses.definition: at the appropriate or expected time, without rushing or delay

examples.x: The details of the project will be revealed to the team in due course.
Please be patient; your request will be processed in due time.
The project will be completed in due course, according to the schedule.
You will receive a response to your inquiry in due time.
The product will be available for purchase in due course; please check back later.

senses.definition: continuing the whole year

examples.x: She embarked on a yearlong journey around the world to explore different cultures.
The company offered a yearlong internship program for recent graduates.
The project required a yearlong commitment from all team members to ensure its success.
He enrolled in a yearlong language course to become fluent in Spanish.
The school implemented a yearlong study program to improve student performance in mathematics.

senses.definition: a certain or exact point in time

examples.x: He knew at that instant that he had made the right decision.
I need you to finish this task right this instant!
Come here this instant, or you’ll miss the surprise!
She realized in that instant how much the situation had changed.

senses.definition: a short period of time of something particular

examples.x: After a brief spell of rain, the sun came out again.
She experienced a dizzy spell and had to sit down.
During his time in the hospital, he had several spells of dizziness.
The company faced a difficult spell of financial instability but eventually recovered.

senses.definition: a specific period of time in history or in someone's life

examples.x: The turbulent chapter of the nation's history was characterized by civil unrest and political upheaval.
After completing his education, he entered a new chapter of his career, filled with opportunities and challenges.
The chapter of her childhood spent in the quaint village was filled with fond memories of simplicity and innocence.
As she turned the page to a new chapter in her life, she felt a sense of excitement and anticipation for what lay ahead.
The chapter of their relationship was filled with both joyous moments and heartbreaking setbacks.

senses.definition: the time after sun sets that is not yet completely dark

examples.x: As the sky turned a deep shade of orange, signaling the onset of dusk, the city's lights began to flicker on, casting a warm glow over the streets.
The tranquil beauty of the lakeside at dusk, with the sun dipping below the horizon, painted the sky in hues of pink and purple.
The garden took on a magical aura at dusk, with fireflies dancing among the flowers and the scent of jasmine lingering in the air.
At dusk, the bustling marketplace transformed into a quiet, peaceful haven as vendors closed up shop and families headed home for the evening.
The beach was deserted at dusk, save for a few solitary figures walking along the shoreline, silhouetted against the fading light of the sun.

senses.definition: time that is endless

examples.x: Gazing up at the starry night sky, he felt as though he could get lost in the vastness of eternity.
The ancient ruins stood as silent witnesses to the passage of eternity, their weathered stones bearing the marks of countless generations.
Love, they say, lasts for an eternity, transcending the boundaries of time and space.
In the quiet solitude of the forest, time seemed to stand still, as if caught in the embrace of eternity.
As the sun dipped below the horizon, painting the sky in shades of pink and gold, she felt a sense of peace wash over her, a fleeting glimpse of eternity.

senses.definition: the middle part of summer when it is hottest

examples.x: The joyous celebrations of midsummer echoed through the quaint village, where families gathered to revel in the warmth of the season.
As the sun lingered in the sky during midsummer, casting a golden glow across the meadows, children played and laughter filled the air.
In the heart of midsummer, the vibrant colors of blooming flowers painted a picturesque landscape, creating a scene of natural beauty.

senses.definition: the middle part of winter when it is coldest

examples.x: As midwinter descended upon the land, the trees stood bare against the gray sky, their branches etched in delicate patterns of frost.
The howling winds of midwinter swept across the frozen landscape, carrying with them the whispers of ancient tales and the promise of eventual spring.
In the stillness of midwinter nights, the stars shone with a crystalline brilliance, casting their silvery light upon the silent world below.
As midwinter reached its zenith, the landscape transformed into a realm of ice and shadow, where time seemed to stand still beneath the cold, starlit heavens.
The beauty of midwinter lay in its stark simplicity, as nature pared down to its most essential elements and revealed the quiet resilience of life.

senses.definition: either of the two times of the year when the sun reaches its farthest or closest distance from the equator
a period of three months, typically used in financial contexts

examples.x: The company's profits increased during the second quarter of the year.
Investors closely monitor earnings during the first quarter.
The third quarter is usually the busiest for retail businesses.
As the first quarter comes to an end, organizations take stock of their progress against annual goals.
The company reported strong earnings in the third quarter of the year.

senses.definition: a year in every four years that has 366 days instead of 365

examples.x: 2020 was a leap year, so it had 366 days instead of the usual 365.
We celebrate my birthday every leap year on February 29th.
The next leap year will be in 2024.
Because it's a leap year, February will have 29 days instead of 28.
Leap years help to keep our calendar synchronized with the seasons.

senses.definition: done or happening every other time
happening repeatedly or continuously in an annoying or problematic way

examples.x: The continual noise from the construction site disrupted the neighborhood's peace.
His continual interruptions during the meeting hindered progress on the project.
The continual rain caused flooding in low-lying areas.
She faced continual challenges in her efforts to launch her own business.
The continual delays in the train schedule frustrated commuters.

senses.definition: continuously happening one after another

examples.x: He scored three consecutive goals in the match, leading his team to victory.
The company reported consecutive quarterly losses, leading to concerns among investors.
This is the fifth consecutive day of heavy rainfall in the region.
She has won the championship for three consecutive years.
The team has suffered consecutive defeats, putting their playoff hopes in jeopardy.

senses.definition: happening one after another, in an uninterrupted sequence
continuing or existing forever

examples.x: Love is often described as an eternal bond that transcends time and space.
The beauty of nature's landscapes seems eternal, untouched by the passage of years.
Some believe in the eternal soul, a spiritual essence that outlasts the physical body.
Time itself is often portrayed as an eternal force, ceaselessly marching forward.
The poet penned verses about the eternal mysteries of the universe, pondering questions that defy human understanding.

senses.definition: happening at the end of a process or a particular period of time

examples.x: After years of hard work and dedication, he finally achieved his eventual goal of becoming a successful entrepreneur.
Despite facing numerous obstacles along the way, she remained determined to reach her eventual destination.
The eventual outcome of the negotiations was a mutually beneficial agreement for all parties involved.
With careful planning and strategic decision-making, they were able to anticipate and prepare for their eventual success.
Although the road ahead may be challenging, they remain optimistic about their eventual triumph.

senses.definition: (particularly of something unpleasant) likely to take place in the near future

examples.x: With tensions escalating between the two countries, war seemed imminent.
As the volcano continued to show signs of activity, residents feared an imminent eruption.
The doctor warned the patient of the imminent risk of heart attack if lifestyle changes were not made.
Despite efforts to contain the virus, health officials warned of the imminent spread of the disease.
The soldiers braced for the imminent attack from the enemy forces.

senses.definition: continuing for too long

examples.x: The meeting was unnecessarily lengthy, dragging on for hours without reaching any conclusive decisions.
The novel was criticized for its lengthy descriptions, which slowed down the pacing of the story.
The movie's lengthy runtime tested the audience's patience, leading to some viewers becoming restless.
Despite efforts to condense the presentation, it still ended up being overly lengthy and exhausting.
The project's timeline had to be extended due to a series of lengthy delays in the development phase.

senses.definition: happening or done from time to time, without a consistent pattern

examples.x: She enjoys the occasional glass of wine with dinner.
Tom's occasional visits to his hometown always bring back fond memories of his childhood.
We have occasional power outages in our neighborhood, usually during severe storms.
Despite her occasional absences from class, Maria always manages to catch up on her studies.
The old bookstore hosts occasional book signings with renowned authors to attract customers.
His visits to the gym were only occasional, despite his goal of getting fit.
The occasional email from an old friend brightened up her day.

senses.definition: likely to become a reality in the future

examples.x: The prospective merger between the two companies has generated a lot of interest in the business community.
After several rounds of interviews, Sarah received a job offer from her prospective employer.
Prospective buyers toured the property over the weekend, evaluating its potential as an investment.
The prospective candidate impressed the hiring committee with her qualifications and experience.
The real estate agent provided a virtual tour of the prospective home to interested buyers.

senses.definition: taking place at precisely the same time

examples.x: The two teams scored simultaneous goals, resulting in a tie game.
Simultaneous translation services were provided for attendees from different countries.
The company launched simultaneous marketing campaigns across multiple platforms to maximize reach.
The orchestra and choir performed simultaneous pieces, showcasing their synchronization and skill.
The conference featured simultaneous translation into multiple languages to accommodate international attendees.

senses.definition: closest to the end of a particular period of time, event, etc.
occurring every night

examples.x: She had a nightly routine of taking a warm bath before bed to help her promote relaxation.

senses.definition: appearing, made, or happening once a year

examples.x: The company holds a yearly conference to discuss new developments and strategies.
The town's yearly festival attracts visitors from far and wide.
The yearly tradition of family reunions brings relatives together from across the country.
The museum hosts a yearly exhibit showcasing local artists' work.
The yearly flu shot is recommended for individuals at high risk of infection.

senses.definition: happening the whole year

examples.x: The resort offers year-round activities, including skiing in the winter and hiking in the summer.
The beach is a popular destination for year-round sunbathing and swimming.
The museum offers year-round exhibits and events to engage visitors of all ages.
The resort boasts year-round amenities, making it a popular destination for all seasons.
The company provides year-round employment opportunities, offering stability for its workers.

senses.definition: a period of time added to something to prolong it

examples.x: The professor granted an extension on the deadline for the research paper due to unforeseen circumstances.
The company announced an extension of the sale, allowing customers more time to take advantage of discounted prices.
The government approved an extension of the tax filing deadline due to technical issues with the online portal.
The landlord agreed to a lease extension, giving the tenants another year in the rental property.
The athlete requested an extension of the training camp to further prepare for the upcoming competition.

senses.definition: non-stop and continuing through the whole day and night

examples.x: The customer service hotline operates around the clock to assist customers at any time.
They work around the clock to meet the project deadline.
The security team patrols the premises around the clock to ensure safety.
The team worked around the clock to complete the construction project ahead of schedule.
The emergency response team operated around the clock during the natural disaster.

senses.definition: a point in time when something begins or is started

examples.x: Down from the get-go, there was no question about his ability.
I've been down for the West side since the get-go.
She didn't like me from the get-go.
They were involved in the project right from the get-go.

senses.definition: having persisted or existed for a significant amount of time

examples.x: Their longstanding friendship began in elementary school and has endured through all the ups and downs of life.
The company has a longstanding tradition of community involvement and philanthropy.
This longstanding dispute between the two nations dates back centuries and shows no sign of resolution.
The restaurant is known for its longstanding commitment to using locally sourced ingredients in its dishes.

senses.definition: (of a thing) having existed or been in use for a significant period of time

examples.x: Our family's longtime tradition of gathering every Sunday remains intact.
The organization honored its longtime volunteers with a special recognition ceremony.
The museum finally decided to retire its longtime exhibit after decades of display.
Their longtime partnership in business has been highly successful.
They have shared a longtime friendship that has withstood the test of time.

senses.definition: used to refer to a date that is after the birth of Jesus Christ
used when something happens almost at the same time as another

examples.x: He praises her talent, but in the same breath, he criticizes her lack of experience.
They advocate for environmental sustainability, but in the next breath, they support policies that harm the environment.
He praised her work, but in the same breath, he mentioned her previous mistakes.
They enjoyed the concert, but in the next breath, they complained about the sound quality.
They were talking about the benefits of the new system, but in the next breath, they were criticizing its limitations.

senses.definition: marking the years before Christ's supposed birth
used with a date to refer to things happened or existed after the birth of Christ
